description: Anti-double-count regression fixture for AC-5 (feature-plans/walk-forward-overhaul.md Phase 2). compute_n_effective output must be identical whether CPCV uses 1 path or 5 paths. CPCV changes WHAT data each trial is scored on, not HOW MANY tests exist. Hardcoding n_paths into n_effective would inflate N by C(N,k) -- the forbidden pattern.

single_path_n_effective.description: CPCV with 1 path (degenerate): n_effective must equal n_optuna (S=0, NN1-honest)
single_path_n_effective.rationale: S=0 (empty ledger), so n_effective = n_optuna regardless of n_cpcv_paths

five_path_n_effective.description: CPCV with 5 paths (production): n_effective must STILL equal n_optuna (S=0, NN1-honest)
five_path_n_effective.rationale: S=0 (empty ledger), so n_effective = n_optuna regardless of n_cpcv_paths

fifteen_splits_would_be_wrong.description: If n_effective were inflated by C(N,k)=15, this would be the wrong value
fifteen_splits_would_be_wrong.rationale: CPCV does NOT multiply the number of statistical tests. The 15 splits are the same 500 Optuna trials scored on different data windows. n_effective = n_optuna + S (from researcher DOF ledger), never *= n_splits or n_paths.
